2604.12373.title: Masked by Consensus: Disentangling Privileged Knowledge in LLM Correctness
2604.12373.paper_summary: Humans use introspection to evaluate their understanding through private internal states inaccessible to external observers. We investigate whether large language models possess similar privileged knowledge about answer correctness, information unavailable through external observation. We train correctness classifiers on question representations from both a model's own hidden states and external models, testing whether self-representations provide a performance advantage. On standard evaluation, we find no advantage: self-probes perform comparably to peer-model probes. We hypothesize this is due to high inter-model agreement of answer correctness. To isolate genuine privileged knowledge, we evaluate on disagreement subsets, where models produce conflicting predictions. Here, we discover domain-specific privileged knowledge: self-representations consistently outperform peer representations in factual knowledge tasks, but show no advantage in math reasoning. We further localize this domain asymmetry across model layers, finding that the factual advantage emerges progressively from early-to-mid layers onward, consistent with model-specific memory retrieval, while math reasoning shows no consistent advantage at any depth.
2604.12373.paper_summary_zh: 人類使用內省來評估他們的理解，透過對外部觀察者無法接觸的私密內部狀態。我們調查大型語言模型是否擁有類似的特權知識，關於答案的正確性，這些信息無法通過外部觀察獲得。我們在問題表示上訓練正確性分類器，這些表示來自模型自身的隱藏狀態和外部模型，測試自我表示是否提供性能優勢。在標準評估中，我們發現沒有優勢：自我探測的表現與同儕模型探測相當。我們假設這是由於答案正確性之間的高一致性。為了隔離真正的特權知識，我們在不一致的子集上進行評估，這些子集中的模型產生相互矛盾的預測。在這裡，我們發現了領域特定的特權知識：在事實知識任務中，自我表示始終優於同儕表示，但在數學推理中則沒有優勢。我們進一步定位這種領域不對稱性在模型層之間，發現事實優勢逐漸從早期到中層出現，這與模型特定的記憶檢索一致，而數學推理在任何深度都沒有一致的優勢。
2604.12373.author: Tomer Ashuach et.al.
2604.12373.authors: Tomer Ashuach, Liat Ein-Dor, Shai Gretz, Yoav Katz, Yonatan Belinkov
